nameMeanings.name: Gordon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Gordon in Berwickshire, named with Welsh gor ‘spacious’ + din ‘fort’.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Gourdon in Saône-et-Loire, so called from the Gallo-Roman personal name Gordus + the locative suffix -o, -ōnis.
English (of Norman origin): alternatively, said to be a nickname from a diminutive of Old French gourd ‘heavy, dull, sluggish’ (compare 8 below).

variants.name: Gordin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Eyal, Moisey, Yakov, Zalman, Erez, Faina, Haya, Mendel.  Russian Leonid, Aleksandr, Boris, Feiga, Genady.
French: variant of   Gourdin  . This surname is very rare in France.

variants.name: Gordan
meanings: German: variant of Jordan . The surname Gordan is very rare in Germany.
Irish (Wexford and Sligo): adopted as an Anglicized form of Mag Mhuirneacháin ‘son of Muirneachán’, a personal name from a diminutive of muirneach ‘lovable’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Gordon was born as the daughter of Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland and Lady Annabella Stewart Princess of Scotland. She married Patrick Hepburn 1st Earl of Bothwell on 21 February 1491. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Gordon est née, fille de Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland et Lady Annabella Stewart Princess of Scotland. Elle a épousé Patrick Hepburn 1st Earl of Bothwell le 21 février 1491. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Gordon wurde, als Tochter von Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland und Lady Annabella Stewart Princess of Scotland geboren. Sie hat am 21. Februar 1491 Patrick Hepburn 1st Earl of Bothwell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Gordon è nata il come figlia di Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland e Lady Annabella Stewart Princess of Scotland. Lei sposato Patrick Hepburn 1st Earl of Bothwell il 21 febbraio 1491. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret GordonはSir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of ScotlandとLady Annabella Stewart Princess of Scotlandの娘として生まれた。1491年2月21日にPatrick Hepburn 1st Earl of Bothwellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Gordon(은)는 Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland(와)과 Lady Annabella Stewart Princess of Scotland의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1491년 2월 21일 Patrick Hepburn 1st Earl of Bothwell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Gordon родилась. Её родителей звали Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland и Lady Annabella Stewart Princess of Scotland. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Patrick Hepburn 1st Earl of Bothwell 21 февраля 1491 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret Gordon nació como hija de Sir George Gordon 2nd Earl of Huntly -  High Chancellor of Scotland y Lady Annabella Stewart Princess of Scotland. Se casó con Patrick Hepburn 1st Earl of Bothwell el 21 de febrero de 1491. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija.
